hero.title: My time is not mine
hero.subtitle: The right to independent living is also the right to your own time, and barriers keep taking it away.
hero.description: Part of KEEP Driven, an Erasmus+ project on participation co-financed by the European Union, where young people with disabilities from Italy, France and Latvia shaped a campaign on independent living.

campaign.title: Our time belongs to us
campaign.lead: Drag or swipe to explore the campaign visuals. Tap a card to read the full story.

demand.body: We want to decide how we live our daily lives. We want support service systems that adapt to our rhythms. We want accessible transport, flexible education and work opportunities, and the right to be heard when decisions about our lives are made. Our time belongs to us.
cards: We want to decide how we live our daily lives, without having to depend on other people's schedules.
We want support services that give us freedom and autonomy, adapt to our rhythms and health conditions, and allow us to take care of ourselves.
We want laws that truly guarantee independent living and are fully implemented in every country.
We want accessible public transport and support services that allow us to study, work, meet friends and participate fully in society.
We want flexible opportunities in work and education, without being pushed into isolation or excluded from participation.
We want to be heard and involved when decisions about our lives are made, because our experiences must help shape the society around us.
We want an equitable system for people with disabilities in Europe: a system that gives us choice, control and ownership over our own time and lives.

keepDriven.title: What is KEEP Driven
keepDriven.lead: A pan-European advocacy project where young people with disabilities lead the way: training, study visits and collaborative workshops with participants from Italy, France, Belgium and Latvia.

stats.title: Countries
stats.text: Italy, France, Latvia and Belgium working together.

stats.title: People
stats.text: Expected to be reached through the campaign.

keepDriven.behindText: Young people with disabilities aged 18–26 from Italy, France and Latvia, with a priority on women. They are the authors and advocates of this campaign, not its subjects.

journey.title: Our journey
journey.lead: From experience to action: how young people with disabilities became the authors of a European advocacy campaign.

items.title: Project launch
items.text: Communication plan, visual identity and landing page go live.

items.title: Study visits
items.text: Young participants travel across Italy, Latvia and France, exchanging knowledge with local disability organisations and documenting lived experiences.

items.title: Co-creating the campaign
items.text: Participants work together to design and develop the advocacy campaign, messages, visuals and strategy, for a European audience.

items.title: Campaign launch
items.text: The advocacy campaign is launched at European level, with national events in Italy, France and Latvia.

positionPaper.title: Not only visibility. Political change.
positionPaper.lead: Young people with disabilities must be involved in the decisions that shape their future.

takePart.title: Share your story. Join the conversation.
takePart.text: Have you ever felt that barriers, systems or inaccessible spaces were taking your time away? Join the campaign by spreading the word on social media.

gallery.title: Behind the campaign
gallery.lead: A visual archive of the campaign documenting the people and conversations behind “My time is not mine”.

partners.title: Partnership
partners.lead: An Erasmus+ partnership coordinated by CBM Italia, bringing together five organisations committed to disability inclusion and youth empowerment.
partners.fundingText: Co-funded by the European Union. Views and opinions expressed are those of the author(s) only and do not necessarily reflect those of the EU or EACEA.
